page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-176-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C processors. It features 176 virtual CPUs across 88 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 704.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is a key feature of this configuration, which bundles 12599 GB of NVMe SSD capacity across 32 local drives. There are no GPUs included in this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU testing and Passmark CPU scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The Intel Xeon processor's support for Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) contributes to top-tier results in extended instruction and encryption tests. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, registering top-tier read and write speeds for large blocks. However, single-core performance remains in the average tier, as shown by stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core results.

This server is designed for high-density workloads that require substantial local storage and high parallel processing capabilities. The combination of a massive 12.5 TB NVMe SSD array and strong multi-core performance makes it suitable for large-scale database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing. While the instance lacks GPU acceleration, its AMX support enables strong performance in CPU-based LLM inference prompt processing. The high resource density of 176 dedicated vCPUs and extensive local storage offers qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise-scale deployments that would otherwise require multiple smaller nodes.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-176-lssd is an x86_64 server featuring 176 dedicated vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8481C processors running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 704.0 GB of RAM. This configuration includes 12599 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 32 drives, but lacks GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, alongside strong database and web serving throughput. Conversely, single-core processing speeds fall into the average tier. The server is optimized for highly parallelized workloads, large-scale database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference. Its high resource density provides qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise deployments requiring massive local storage and high-throughput multi-threaded execution.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-176-lssd features 176 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 704 GB RAM, and 12599 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-176-lssd is an x86_64 server with 176 vCPUs, 704 GB RAM, and 12.5 TB NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-density x86_64 server combining 176 dedicated vCPUs with 12.5 TB of local NVMe SSD storage for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 176 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors at 2.7 GHz.
704 GB of system memory providing 4 GB of RAM per core.
12599 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 32 bundled drives.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth in benchmark testing.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated cryptography and vector instructions.
Well-suited for large-scale database operations, web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
